name: Dify MCP Plugin Server
what_it_does: Official Dify MCP plugin server enabling AI agents to interact with Dify — the open-source LLM application development platform — querying Dify workflows and chatbots, triggering automated pipelines, accessing Dify knowledge bases, managing applications, and integrating Dify's no-code/low-code AI application infrastructure into agent-driven workflows.
use_cases: Triggering Dify workflow automations from orchestration agents
Querying Dify chatbot applications for specialized domain knowledge from agent systems
Accessing Dify knowledge bases for RAG retrieval from information agents
Building multi-agent systems where Dify apps serve as specialized sub-agents
Managing Dify application configurations from infrastructure agents
Integrating existing Dify pipelines into larger agent workflows from automation agents
not_for: Teams not using Dify (use LangChain, LlamaIndex, or other LLM framework MCPs)
Building new AI apps from scratch (use Dify directly rather than via MCP)
High-throughput production workloads without Dify enterprise plan
best_when: An agent needs to invoke Dify-built AI workflows or applications as sub-components — Dify's visual workflow builder creates applications that can be triggered via this MCP.
avoid_when: You don't use Dify or prefer other LLM application frameworks like LangChain or LlamaIndex.

auth.notes: Dify API key required. Self-hosted Dify or Dify Cloud account needed. API key generated per Dify application.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Dify Cloud: free sandbox plan with limited messages; self-hosted is free
pricing.paid_tiers: Dify Cloud Pro/Enterprise for production use
pricing.notes: Dify is open source — self-hosted is completely free. Dify Cloud has a free sandbox. MCP plugin is free. Production Dify Cloud requires paid plan.

performance.rate_limits: Dify API: plan-dependent rate limits; self-hosted has no limits

known_agent_gotchas: Dify applications must be built and deployed before they can be invoked via MCP
Dify is a fast-moving project — API may change significantly between versions
Self-hosted Dify requires proper infrastructure setup (Docker, Postgres, Redis)
Official Dify plugin MCP but Dify ecosystem matures rapidly — verify current API
Dify Cloud rate limits in free tier are quite low — may hit limits quickly
LLM costs in Dify workflows billed to the Dify account's configured providers
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS for Dify Cloud. API key auth. Self-hosted: restrict network access. Dify processes data via configured LLM providers — review each provider's policies. Official Dify MCP.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS. API key. LLM provider policies apply. Official Dify MCP. Self-hosted option available.
